summary.name: Systematic SPY Wheel Premium Harvesting
summary.author: Derek Melchin

summary.description_full:
The strategy implements a systematic covered option premium program on SPY designed to monetize option implied volatility and time decay while rotating exposure between cash secured puts and covered calls (a wheel style approach). When the portfolio has no positions and SPY is trading, it sells a single SPY put with approximately one month to expiry (the nearest listed expiry beyond 30 calendar days) and a strike about 5 percent below spot, targeting equity acquisition at a discount while collecting premium. If the put is assigned and SPY shares are held, the strategy transitions to selling call options with the same tenor and a strike about 5 percent above spot, aiming to harvest additional premium and potentially exit the equity via call exercise at an appreciated level.

Decisions are evaluated on each incoming data update during market hours using the current SPY spot price and the live option chain. Position sizing is constant notional at initiation, targeting roughly 20 percent of portfolio exposure in the short option, and the covered call quantity is set to match the existing share position at one contract per 100 shares. Primary risks are equity gap risk on short puts, capped upside and continued downside on the covered equity plus short call, and assignment and liquidity effects around expiries.

raw.name: Systematic SPY Wheel Premium Harvesting

author.name: Derek Melchin
author.bio:
Quantitative Developer at QuantConnect. Studied Computer Science and Finance at the University of Lethbridge. Competitor in the 2020-1 Rotman International Trading Competitions. 
See my latest posts at derekmelchin.com.

raw.description:
The strategy implements a systematic covered option premium program on SPY designed to monetize option implied volatility and time decay while rotating exposure between cash secured puts and covered calls (a wheel style approach). When the portfolio has no positions and SPY is trading, it sells a single SPY put with approximately one month to expiry (the nearest listed expiry beyond 30 calendar days) and a strike about 5 percent below spot, targeting equity acquisition at a discount while collecting premium. If the put is assigned and SPY shares are held, the strategy transitions to selling call options with the same tenor and a strike about 5 percent above spot, aiming to harvest additional premium and potentially exit the equity via call exercise at an appreciated level.

Decisions are evaluated on each incoming data update during market hours using the current SPY spot price and the live option chain. Position sizing is constant notional at initiation, targeting roughly 20 percent of portfolio exposure in the short option, and the covered call quantity is set to match the existing share position at one contract per 100 shares. Primary risks are equity gap risk on short puts, capped upside and continued downside on the covered equity plus short call, and assignment and liquidity effects around expiries.
